safety_signals.meaning: attack/defense bonuses active; unit recovery proceeding

safety_signals.meaning: tactical advantage (e.g., cavalry vs soldiers, holy vs demons)

safety_signals.visual_form: commander within unit formation using Heal/Treat command
safety_signals.meaning: 3 HP recovery + 2 MP per turn for affected units

world_state_to_visual.world_state: player faction alignment (Der Langrisser branching paths)

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: unit availability changes; ally relationships shift; scenario pathways alter based on player choices

notes: Warsong (1991) is the North American Genesis release of Langrisser I. The game communicates world-state primarily through tactical positioning, unit type affinity relationships, and religious/metaphysical faction symbolism (Chaos vs Light). Danger and safety are primarily mechanically rather than visually signaled—danger emerges from unit disadvantage, isolation, and affinity vulnerability; safety from commander proximity, favorable matchups, and healing positioning. The branching faction system (introduced in Der Langrisser, the sequel) enables political world-state communication through ally availability and scenario access. Command radius as a visual mechanic is central to communicating authority and protection zones.
